source.title: Ventral hippocampus interacts with prelimbic cortex during inhibition of threat response via learned safety in both mice and humans
source.venue: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences

confidence_rationale: Cross-species (mouse chemogenetics + human fMRI) convergent demonstration that conditioned inhibition via safety learning routes through a vHipp-to-PL pathway, dissociable from amygdala connectivity. Reinforces the Kreutzmann 2020 reading that safety-cue prediction has its own substrate. Direction is 'weakens' for the strong Model 1 reading (single-pipeline reuse) and supports the hybrid recommendation.
failure_signatures: An REE substrate that models 'predicting where relief will come from' as the same mechanism that handles 'this thing just made suffering drop' will lose the architectural distinction between the safety-prediction circuit (vHipp -> PL) and the relief-completion circuit (BA -> NAcSh).

The cross-species mapping is partial — humans show hippocampal-dorsal-anterior-cingulate connectivity rather than literal hippocampal-PL connectivity, which the authors translate via homology rather than direct anatomical match.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Ventral hippocampal neurons projecting to the prelimbic cortex (but not to the infralimbic cortex or basolateral amygdala) are more active to safety and compound (threat+safety) cues than to threat cues, and their activity correlates with freezing behaviour. In humans, hippocampal-dorsal-ACC functional connectivity (but not hippocampal-vmPFC or hippocampal-BLA connectivity) differentiates threat, safety, and compound conditions.
mapping.ree_translation: Conditioned inhibition of threat via learned safety routes through a hippocampus-to-prefrontal pathway that is anatomically distinct from both the amygdalar threat circuit and the ventral-striatal relief-completion circuit. For REE this strengthens the case (also made by Kreutzmann 2020 for IL) that the predictive encoding of safety is a parallel substrate, not a polarity-flip of the goal-achievement encoder. The architectural decision should be hybrid: completion event reuses goal-achievement machinery; safety-cue prediction has its own learned-prediction structure.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The species mapping is imperfect — mouse vHipp -> PL maps to human hippocampus -> dACC rather than hippocampus -> mPFC, and the precise homology of PL/dACC across species is debated. The convergent dissociation across both species is the load-bearing finding rather than the literal anatomical match.
mapping.source_context: Adult C57BL/6 mice with chemogenetic activation of vHipp projection-defined cell populations + adult human participants in fMRI; conditioned inhibition paradigm with shape/colour cues.

confidence_components.notes: PNAS, cross-species design with chemogenetic specificity in the rodent and matched task in humans. Transfer risk reduced by the species convergence.
